name: long_page_with_urls
raw_text:
FNDR architecture overview

FNDR is a privacy-first local memory app. The capture pipeline writes OCR text into LanceDB, which is the durable source of truth for all retrieval routes. The embedding contract has two tiers: v4 MiniLM 384 remains the readable parent fallback, and v5 BGE 1024 is the additive forward target for chunk-first retrieval.

https://github.com/fndr-team/fndr

The agentic graph RAG layer composes typed graph paths over the LanceDB store. Each memory carries a deterministic surfacing reason that explains why it appeared for a given query. Chunk evidence is attached to the SearchResult when the chunk route contributes to fusion.

https://lancedb.com/docs

The meeting capture pipeline isolates a transcript per session and emits segment-level memories. Each segment is independently chunked so a single phrase that appears mid-meeting can still surface through chunk-first ANN.

https://docs.fndr.local/meetings
